title: Explicit heat-equation weights are nonnegative under the parabolic CFL condition

source.citation: Randall J. LeVeque, Finite Difference Methods for Ordinary and Partial Differential Equations, SIAM, 2007, Ch. 9.
source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: If r is nonnegative and 2r <= 1, then the three explicit heat-equation weights r, 1 - 2r, and r are all nonnegative.
informal.latex: If \(0\le r\) and \(2r\le1\), then \(0\le r\), \(0\le1-2r\), and \(0\le r\).
assumptions: r is a real diffusion number
informal.expected_conclusion: All three heat-equation update weights are nonnegative.

declarations.name: heatWeights_nonneg
declarations.statement: theorem heatWeights_nonneg (r : Real) (hr0 : 0 <= r) (hrCfl : 2 * r <= 1) : And (0 <= heatLeftWeight r) (And (0 <= heatCenterWeight r) (0 <= heatRightWeight r))

tasks.input: Translate this explicit heat-equation finite-difference theorem into Lean 4: If r is nonnegative and 2r <= 1, then the three explicit heat-equation weights r, 1 - 2r, and r are all nonnegative.
tasks.target: theorem heatWeights_nonneg (r : Real) (hr0 : 0 <= r) (hrCfl : 2 * r <= 1) : And (0 <= heatLeftWeight r) (And (0 <= heatCenterWeight r) (0 <= heatRightWeight r))

tasks.input: Given the heat-equation scaffold definitions and theorem statement, complete the Lean 4 proof.
tasks.target: A proof by unfolding the weight definitions, splitting conjunctions, and using linarith.
